title: rosa canina subsp. glauca
intro: Rosa canina subsp. glauca, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of flowering shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and vitamin C. Rosa canina subsp. glauca has been traditionally used to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce the risk of certain diseases, including cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and capsules, and its hips, which are the rose fruits, can be eaten fresh or dried to make jams, syrups, and other products.
